channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
item.description: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

item.title: Google says it is ‘proud’ to serve the Pentagon – new DoD contract expansion says Gemini will only be used for ‘any lawful purpose’, but what happened to 'Don’t Be Evil'?
item.description: Google's contract with the Pentagon allows the DoD to use Gemini for 'any lawful purpose', and that is a big ethical concern.

item.title: The first Tesla Semi finally rolls off the production line — 7 years after Elon Musk unveiled the long-haul EVs
item.description: It might be seven years later than planned, but the first Tesla Semi truck rolls off the mass production line

item.title: Chipotle’s new brand chief gave fast-food burgers buzz. Now he’s coming for fast-casual burritos
item.description: Fernando Machado is best known for his stunt-driven Burger King work. Will his irreverence find success at Chipotle? Chipotle Mexican Grill needed to do something. In February, the fast-casual restaurant brand reported that traffic to its restaurants fell for the fourth straight quarter to end 2025, and it was projecting flat same-store sales growth for 2026. At that point, the company’s stock had dipped by about 33% over the last year. ...

item.title: 'It quickly becomes an efficient and economically feasible solution': This wild saltwater hack could make data centers cooler, cheaper, and unexpectedly water-positive
item.description: A salt-based cooling system captures waste heat from data centers to generate water, reduce energy use, and improve efficiency metrics.

item.title: Roblox’s daily users continue to drop as age-checks slow growth
item.description: Roblox's daily active users continued to slip last quarter due in part to its rollout of age checks on its platform. According to its latest earnings report, Roblox currently has 132 million daily active users globally, down from 144 million at the end of last year, which was a drop from 152 million in Q3 […]

item.title: Meta says it may withdraw its apps from New Mexico if judge agrees to the state's demands
item.description: Welcome to the Land of Enchantment.

item.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
item.description: Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy […]

item.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
item.description: Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. In an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts," but there's "a little less flexibility at the moment for getting more parts." Cook added during an […]
